title: Off-Campus Student Housing in Cleveland
metaDescription: Looking for off-campus student housing in Cleveland near CWRU or CSU? See rent ranges, whole-house group rentals, and lease basics. Contact us to ask.
secondaryKeywords: off campus housing near case western
answerBox: Off-campus student housing in Cleveland generally means renting a house or apartment near Case Western Reserve University (University Circle) or Cleveland State University (Downtown) instead of a dorm. Rent Finder Cleveland works with standard whole-house and multi-unit rental homes on the east side that can work for student groups splitting rent, though our homes are leased under standard 12-month residential terms, not dorm-style contracts.

sections.h2: What are the main off-campus options near Cleveland's universities?
body: Cleveland's two largest universities anchor different parts of the city. Case Western Reserve University sits within University Circle on the east side, an area also home to the Cleveland Museum of Art, Severance Music Center, and University Hospitals, served by the RTA Red Line and the HealthLine bus rapid transit route along Euclid Avenue. Cleveland State University sits Downtown, near Public Square and the Warehouse District, also served by RTA rail and bus lines converging at the Tower City hub.

Off-campus student housing near either school generally falls into two categories: purpose-built student apartment complexes marketed directly to undergraduates, and standard rental houses or duplexes that a group of students rents together and splits the cost of — the second category is where a local rental team like us fits in.

sections.h2: How can Rent Finder Cleveland's rental homes work for a student group?
body: We work with 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, a portion of which are 3-, 4-, and even 5-bedroom houses and duplex units — sizes that commonly suit a small group of students splitting one lease rather than a single tenant. Several of our homes sit in Hough (ZIP 44103), which is between Downtown and University Circle, putting them within reach of both CSU and CWRU along the same transit corridor.

Every applicant on a shared lease still goes through our standard screening and application process — we do not offer a separate 'student lease' product, but a group of qualified co-tenants applying together for a larger unit is a normal and common arrangement.

sections.h2: What does a shared student rental typically cost per person?
body: Splitting a larger home among roommates is usually the most cost-effective way for students to rent off-campus. The table below shows typical monthly rent by bedroom count from our available homes, alongside a rough per-person cost if split evenly among that many bedrooms.

headers: Rough cost per person if split by bedroom

sections.h2: What should a student group know before applying together?
body: Every co-tenant on a shared lease is typically held jointly responsible for the full rent and any damage, not just their individual share — so it's worth discussing that arrangement with roommates before applying. We'll also want to see that the household's combined income comfortably covers the rent, as most landlords do, and some student renters use a co-signer or guarantor to help meet that requirement; see our [guide to using a co-signer in Ohio](/cleveland-rentals/guides/cosigner-to-rent-apartment-ohio/) for how that works.

If a full house is more space than your group needs, a single room in a shared house may be a better fit — see our [rooms for rent near Cleveland State](/cleveland-rentals/more/rooms-for-rent-near-cleveland-state/) and [rooms for rent near Case Western](/cleveland-rentals/more/rooms-for-rent-near-case-western/) pages, or our related [student house rental near Case Western](/cleveland-rentals/more/student-house-rental-near-case-western/) page for a closer look at that specific corridor.

sections.h2: How do I check what's currently available?
body: Because availability changes regularly, the most reliable way to see current listings that could work for a student group is to [book a showing](/p/book-showing) or start an [online application](/apply) and tell us your group size and target move-in date, and we'll point you to homes that fit.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland rent houses specifically to student groups?
faq.a: We don't have a separate student-housing product, but a group of qualified co-tenants applying together for one of our larger rental homes — 3-, 4-, or 5-bedroom houses or duplex units — is a normal, common arrangement, and several of our east-side homes are within reach of both CWRU and CSU.

faq.q: How far is Case Western Reserve University from Cleveland State University?
faq.a: Case Western Reserve University sits within University Circle on Cleveland's east side, while Cleveland State University sits Downtown. Both areas are connected along the same general RTA Red Line and HealthLine bus rapid transit corridor, which runs through neighborhoods like Hough between the two campuses.

faq.q: Is everyone on a shared student lease responsible for the full rent?
faq.a: Typically yes — most leases, including ours, hold all co-tenants jointly responsible for the full rent and any damage, not just an individual share. It's worth agreeing on how roommates will split costs and handle a shortfall before signing.

faq.q: Can a student use a co-signer or guarantor to qualify for a rental home?
faq.a: In many cases, yes, depending on the specific property and the household's combined income and screening results. See our Ohio co-signer guide for how that process generally works, and reach out to us directly to ask about a specific listing.

faq.q: What if my group only needs one room instead of a whole house?
faq.a: A single room in a shared house is often a better fit for one or two students rather than an entire group. Check our rooms-for-rent pages for the areas near Cleveland State and Case Western for that narrower option.
